title: Incoherent Interface -- Expansion of %h
body:
### Issue Description

```
[Container]
Volume=%h/.config/[...]:[...]
EnvironmentFile=%h/.config/[...]
```
`EnvironmentFile` is a systemd directive where `%h` will be expanded.
`Volume`, on the other hand, is (as far as I know) parsed by `podman-system-generator` and does not expand `%h`.

So, while `%h` is fine for one directive, the next requires an absolute path.

I think this expects more underlying knowledge from the user than it should.


```yaml
podman version 5.8.2
```

comments.body:
Did you test it?

> EnvironmentFile is a systemd directive where %h will be expanded.

Well no, anything under `[Container]` is quadlet specific and turned into the right podman command.

The %h expansion is always performed by systemd because the quadlet generator just creates the string argument `podman run ... --volume %h...` so systemd will expand that just fine when the service is started.

> Did you test it?

Yeah, of course. But now it indeed seems to work ... I had the impression that it was caused by the toggling between `/home/user` and `%h` -- but apparently not. 🙄

I'm sorry for the confusion!
